You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.
You think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.
You output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.
You verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).
You know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.
You deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern.
Create a new note about quantum computing

<think>
I see the Epistemos landing view. The sidebar shows the notes list with a 'New Note' button at the top. I need to click it to create a new note, then type the title.
</think>

{"action": "click", "selector": "AXButton[@AXTitle='New Note']"}
{"action": "type", "selector": "AXTextField[@AXTitle='Title']", "value": "Quantum Computing Research"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "tab"}
{"action": "type", "selector": "AXTextArea[@AXRole='AXTextArea']", "value": "# Quantum Computing\n\n## Key Concepts\n- Superposition\n- Entanglement\n- Quantum gates"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+s"}

**Assessment:** Note created with title 'Quantum Computing Research' and body content. Saved to vault.

Ask the AI to simplify this note for a beginner

<think>
I see a note open in the editor with technical content. I need to open the AI chat sidebar, then type my request. The AI sidebar toggle is in the toolbar.
</think>

{"action": "click", "selector": "AXButton[@AXTitle='AI Chat Sidebar Toggle']"}
{"action": "type", "selector": "AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']", "value": "Simplify this note for someone who has never studied quantum physics. Use analogies."}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "return"}

**Assessment:** AI chat sidebar opened, query submitted. AI is streaming a simplified version below the divider.

The AI response looks good, accept it

<think>
I see the AI response below the --- divider in the note editor. There are Accept and Discard buttons visible. I'll click Accept to merge the AI text into the note body.
</think>

{"action": "click", "selector": "AXButton[@AXTitle='Accept AI Response']"}

**Assessment:** AI response merged into note body. Divider removed. Note now contains the simplified content inline.

<think>
I see the note editor. I need to switch to the graph view. There should be a graph icon in the toolbar or navigation. The graph will show this note as a central node with its connections.

Search for all notes about machine learning in my vault

<think>
I see the notes sidebar with a search field at the top. I'll focus the search field and type my query. The notes list will filter in real-time as I type.
</think>

{"action": "focus", "selector": "AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']"}
{"action": "type", "selector": "AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']", "value": "machine learning"}

**Assessment:** Search results filtering in real-time. I can see notes containing 'machine learning' in their title or body.

<think>
I see the note editor with a paragraph of text. I need to select the paragraph first, then right-click to get the AI context menu, then choose 'Expand'.

{"action": "click", "selector": "AXTextArea[@AXRole='AXTextArea']"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+a"}
{"action": "click", "selector": "AXTextArea[@AXRole='AXTextArea']", "modifiers": ["ctrl"]}
{"action": "click", "selector": "AXMenuItem[@AXTitle='Expand']"}

**Assessment:** Context menu appeared with AI options. Clicked 'Expand'. AI is now streaming an expanded version below the divider.

Create a new folder called 'Research' on the Desktop

<think>
I need to activate Finder, navigate to the Desktop, then create a new folder. I can use Cmd+Shift+N for new folder, then type the name.
</think>

{"action": "shortcut", "selector": "activate_app", "value": "Finder"}
{"action": "click", "selector": "AXOutline[@AXTitle='Sidebar'] > AXRow[@AXTitle='Desktop']"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+shift+n"}
{"action": "type", "selector": "AXTextField", "value": "Research"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "return"}

Move the file 'notes.txt' to the Research folder

<think>
I see the Desktop in Finder with notes.txt visible. I need to drag it to the Research folder. Alternatively, I can cut and paste: select the file, Cmd+C, navigate to Research, then Cmd+Option+V to move.
</think>

{"action": "click", "selector": "AXCell[@AXTitle='notes.txt']"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+c"}
{"action": "double_click", "selector": "AXCell[@AXTitle='Research']"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+option+v"}

**Assessment:** Selected notes.txt, copied, navigated into Research folder, moved the file. notes.txt is now inside Research/.

Take a screenshot of just this window

<think>
macOS has Cmd+Shift+4 for region screenshot, but Cmd+Shift+4 then Space lets me click a specific window. Even better, Cmd+Shift+5 opens the screenshot toolbar where I can select window capture.
</think>

{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+shift+4"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "space"}
{"action": "click", "selector": ""}

**Assessment:** Screenshot mode activated in window capture mode. Clicked the current window. Screenshot saved to Desktop.

Find the last email from Alice and summarize it in a new Epistemos note

<think>
Multi-app workflow: 1) Open Mail, 2) Search for Alice's emails, 3) Read the latest one, 4) Copy key content, 5) Switch to Epistemos, 6) Create note with summary. Starting with Mail.
</think>

{"action": "shortcut", "selector": "activate_app", "value": "Mail"}
{"action": "focus", "selector": "AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']"}
{"action": "type", "selector": "AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']", "value": "from:Alice"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "return"}
{"action": "click", "selector": "AXRow[0]"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+a"}
{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "cmd+c"}
{"action": "shortcut", "selector": "activate_app", "value": "Epistemos"}
{"action": "click", "selector": "AXButton[@AXTitle='New Note']"}

**Assessment:** Found Alice's email, copied content, created Epistemos note with email summary.
